name: tastytrade Trading API
what_it_does: Brokerage API from tastytrade (formerly tastyworks) providing programmatic access to options, futures, equities, and crypto trading with real-time streaming market data, account management, and order execution focused on derivatives traders.
use_cases: Executing complex options strategies (spreads, condors, strangles) via API for algo traders
Streaming real-time options quotes and greeks for automated derivatives analysis
Managing futures positions and margin across equity futures and micro contracts
Building options screening tools with real-time IV rank and market data
Automating portfolio hedging strategies with options and futures positions
not_for: Simple buy-and-hold equity investing (better free tools exist)
Institutional HFT — tastytrade targets retail derivative traders
best_when: An agent manages a derivatives portfolio with complex options/futures strategies, needing a developer-friendly retail brokerage with strong options focus.
avoid_when: You need institutional prime brokerage, FX trading, or non-US markets.

auth.notes: Session token authentication — login with credentials to receive session token. Tokens expire and require re-authentication. No OAuth2 flow for third-party app authorization. Designed for single-account programmatic access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No separate API fee; requires live tastytrade brokerage account
pricing.paid_tiers: Commission-free equities; $1/contract options (capped at $10/leg); futures commissions apply
pricing.notes: API access comes with opening a tastytrade brokerage account. Low commissions focused on options and futures. Popular among retail derivatives traders. Papertrading not available via API — live account required.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; session-based rate limiting

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation available but less comprehensive than Alpaca or Tradier. Community Python SDK fills gaps. WebSocket streaming for market data. Session token handling adds auth complexity for agents.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Market data reads are idempotent. Order placement is not — duplicate orders possible without client-side deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: Session token authentication means agents must handle re-login on token expiry
No paper trading via API — agents must use live money for testing
Options complex order formatting has strict validation requirements
Rate limits not publicly documented — agents must implement conservative throttling

security_score_components.security_notes: FINRA-regulated, SIPC member, NFA-registered FCM for futures. TLS enforced. Session token authentication (less secure than OAuth2 for long-running agents). Credential handling requires care in agent implementations.
